research.title: One App, Many Frameworks: Astro Islands with React, Vue, and Svelte
research.whyTrending: Astro's ability to run React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid components side by side (with client: directives) is a genuinely unique selling point and a migration lifeline — underexplained in practical, gotcha-aware depth.

secondaryKeywords: astro react vue svelte same page
seo.suggestedTitle: One Astro App, Many Frameworks: React, Vue & Svelte

gaps: Guides show that you CAN mix frameworks but under-sell WHY youd want to (migration, best-tool-per-widget) and the cost (multiple runtimes)
Shared-state gotcha (no React context across islands, Nano Stores) is the real pain and often buried
client: directives explained but not mapped to WHEN each
Each island bundles its own framework runtime -> payload cost of mixing rarely quantified
client:only vs client:load nuance for framework components skipped
strengths: Astro docs canonical on directives + sharing state recipe

officialSources.note: Nano Stores for cross-island/cross-framework shared state
islands hydrate independently, each bundles its runtime

expertInsights: Official integrations: React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, Preact, Alpine - mix on the same page
Each island hydrates independently and bundles its OWN framework runtime -> mixing costs payload
client: directives control hydration: client:load, client:idle, client:visible, client:media, client:only
Shared state across islands can NOT use React/Vue context (islands are isolated) - use Nano Stores
Nano Stores caveats: writing from a .astro/non-hydrated component wont reach client islands; cant pass a store as a prop
Alternatives for cross-island state: Web APIs (localStorage, custom events, shared data attributes)
svelte/store only auto-subscribes inside Svelte; use Nano Stores for cross-framework
Primary real use: incremental migration (React->Svelte piece by piece) and best-tool-per-widget
commonProblems: Assuming React context/Redux works across islands (it doesnt - duplicate fetching, no shared state)
Shipping 3 framework runtimes without realizing the payload cost
Passing a Nano Store as a prop (unsupported)
Reaching for multi-framework when one would do

strategy.angle: Sell the genuinely-unique capability (mix React/Vue/Svelte on one page) honestly: its a migration superpower and a best-tool-per-widget option, but islands are ISOLATED - no shared context, each ships its own runtime. Center the real pain (cross-island/cross-framework shared state via Nano Stores and its caveats) and the payload cost, so readers use it deliberately
strategy.title: One Astro App, Many Frameworks: React, Vue, and Svelte Together
strategy.excerpt: Astro can run React, Vue, and Svelte components side by side on the same page - a genuine migration superpower. But islands are isolated: no shared context, and each ships its own runtime. Heres how to mix frameworks deliberately, share state across them, and not pay for it twice.
hooks: Contrarian: mixing frameworks in Astro is real and useful - and mostly a migration tool, not a lifestyle
Gotcha: your React context and Redux do not cross island boundaries - islands are islands
Cost: every framework you mix ships its own runtime, so best-tool-per-widget isnt free
outline: Hook: React and Svelte on the same page, for real
How it works: integrations and client: directives
The hard truth: islands are isolated
Sharing state across frameworks with Nano Stores
The Nano Stores caveats nobody mentions
When to mix (and when its just showing off)
